summary: The AIM Problem Lists dataset contains 101 open problems from American Institute of Mathematics workshops spanning 80+ topics. Issue tracking shows that all sampled problems currently have 0 open issues and 1 resolved issue each, indicating completed proof attempts, while proof-status metrics (q1–q10) each report 100 issues, suggesting an even, fully-populated evaluation pipeline across the corpus.
problems: Every sampled problem (e.g., aim_addcombapp_0001, aim_addcombapp_0002, aim_hypergraphturan_001) shows 0 open and 1 resolved issue, leaving no live discussion threads to track active research traction.
Many resolved issues carry placeholder titles such as 'Proof of aim_addcombapp_0001: (untitled)', making it impossible to distinguish meaningful results from stub records.
Proof-status fields q1 through q10 each report a uniform 100 issues, a suspiciously flat distribution that likely reflects a default/aggregate value rather than per-problem signal.
Issue-level data is only visible for a small slice of the 101 problems, so traction for the remaining ~94 problems is undetermined.
The 'resolved' status applied uniformly may conflate genuinely closed proofs with automatically generated or imported proof records.
highlights: Strong topical breadth: 101 problems across 80+ areas including spectral theory, combinatorics, number theory, and geometry.
Every sampled problem already has an associated resolved proof issue, indicating mature pipeline coverage rather than a backlog of untouched problems.
The additive combinatorics cluster (aim_addcombapp_*) is well represented and shows consistent proof activity, including a substantively titled result on '3APs in sets of small growth'.
Uniform q1–q10 instrumentation means a complete, comparable evaluation scaffold exists across the dataset.

suggested_todos.title: Backfill descriptive titles for all '(untitled)' resolved proof issues to enable meaningful triage
Audit the uniform q1–q10 = 100 proof-status counts to confirm whether they are real per-problem metrics or a default placeholder
Expand issue visibility to cover all 101 problems so traction can be measured beyond the current sample
Verify that 'resolved' issues represent validated proofs versus auto-imported records, starting with aim_addcombapp_0002 ('3APs in sets of small growth')
Prioritize human review of the additive combinatorics and hypergraph Turan clusters as the most data-complete candidates for near-term solvable write-ups
